data.title: Home Depot-GMS deal gets clearance from Canadian Competition Bureau
data.description:
Home Depot (NYSE:HD) said on Friday that it has obtained clearance from the Canadian Competition Bureau for the proposed acquisition of GMS (NYSE:GMS).

Late June, HD struck a deal to acquire GMS for $110 a share.

Approval from the Canadian Competition Bureau came in the form of a no-action letter.

The no-action letter satisfies the remaining antitrust law-related condition necessary for the proposed acquisition.

The tender offer by Home Depot to acquire all outstanding shares of GMS is set to expire at one minute after 11:59 PM ET on Wednesday, September 3.

data.title: Investors should expect more volatility despite recent record highs - Hennion & Walsh
data.description:
Despite the S&P 500’s (SP500) remarkable rebound from one of the worst starts to the year to record highs, investors should prepare for increased market volatility, according to Kevin Mahn, president and CIO at Hennion & Walsh Asset Management.

“Over the prior 20 weeks, the VIX (VIX) has declined by 69%, the largest volatility crash on record,” Mahn said during an interview with CNBC.

He highlighted that the S&P 500 (SP500) has achieved 20 record highs this year, following 57 record highs last year. But despite trading at a P/E ratio of approximately 26, well above five and ten-year averages, Mahn believes growth opportunities remain in specific sectors.

Investors shouldn’t “flee to the sideline because there’s still more growth opportunities ahead if you follow the money in terms of where all the spending is taking place,” he said, specifically pointing to AI infrastructure (AIEQ), (ARTY) and aerospace and defense (ITA), (XAR), (PPA) as promising areas for investment despite his expectation of short-term volatility.

In addition, he said that a core PCE reading below 3% – the July core CPE came in line at 0.3% MoM and 2.9% YoY – would likely trigger a 25-basis-point interest rate cut in September, while a higher reading would have caused the Fed to pause.

The market has already priced in a September rate cut with 87.1% odds, and another expected in December.

Lastly, Mahn criticized the Federal Reserve’s previous stance, stating they “should have been more accommodative all the way back in June” when they raised unemployment forecasts and lowered GDP growth expectations.

data.title: Super Micro Computer dips after raising concerns about financial controls
data.description:
Shares of Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) fell about 5% on Friday after the company cautioned that weaknesses in its controls related to financial disclosures may, if not remedied, hamper its ability to report results “in a timely and accurate manner.”

"We have identified material weaknesses in our internal control over financial reporting, which could, if not remediated, adversely affect our ability to report our financial condition and results of operations in a timely and accurate manner," said Super Micro in a 10-K regulatory filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

Last year, Super Micro missed an August 2024 deadline to file its annual financial report, and its auditor Ernst & Young resigned in October. However, earlier this year, the company filed its "delinquent" financial reports to the U.S. SEC just ahead of its deadline.

"We have concluded that our internal control over financial reporting was not effective as of June 30, 2025 due to the existence of material weaknesses in such controls," said the server and storage solutions' provider in the 10-K filing on Thursday.

The company added that "we have also concluded that our disclosure controls and procedures were not effective as of June 30, 2025 due to material weaknesses in our internal control over financial reporting."

Super Micro noted that while it has started remediation measures to address the identified material weaknesses, it cannot provide assurance that the efforts will be adequate to allow it to conclude that such controls will be effective in the future.

"We also cannot assure you that additional material weaknesses in our internal control over financial reporting will not arise or be identified in the future," said the company in the filing.

Super Micro did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

data.title: Democratic legislators file bill to legalize cannabis in the U.S.
data.description:
A group of Congressional Democrats led by Rep. Jerrold Nadler (D-NY) on Friday filed the Marijuana Opportunity, Reinvestment, and Expungement (MORE) Act in a bid to legalize cannabis at the federal level.

The legislation seeks to end the prohibition by descheduling cannabis under the Controlled Substances Act (CSA) altogether, amid an ongoing review at the U.S. Drug Enforcement Administration to reschedule marijuana.

Previously, Nadler has filed the legislation in three congressional sessions, and this is his fourth consecutive attempt.

When Republicans were in the majority, the bill didn’t clear the House in the last session but did advance twice while Democrats were in control and Nadler was the chairman of the Judiciary Committee.

The latest version, backed by three dozen co-sponsors, includes provisions to promote equity and address social consequences of criminalization in addition to the descheduling proposal, the cannabis publication Marijuana Moment reported.

It comes after President Donald Trump said early this month that his administration is ready to make a final decision on the review over the next few weeks.

data.title: Consumer sentiment revised down in August, even as inflation expectations ease in
data.description:
August U.S. Consumer Sentiment Index: 58.2 vs. prior reading of 58.6 and 61.7 in July, according to a University of Michigan survey released on Friday.

Year-ahead inflation expectations: +4.8% vs. initial reading of +4.9% in August and +4.5% in July.

Implied five-year inflation: +3.5% vs. +3.9% initial print and +3.4% in July.

Consumer expectations index: 55.9 in August vs. first reading of 57.2 and 57.7 in July.

Current conditions index: 61.7 vs. initial print of 60.9 and 68.0 in prior month.

data.title: Frontier meets with Spirit Air over Spirit's rebuilding efforts - report
data.description:
Spirit Aviation (NYSE:FLYY) has met with Frontier Group (NASDAQ:ULCC) over the airline's rebuilding efforts less than six months after returning from bankruptcy.

Spirit (NYSE:FLYY) Chairman Robert Milton met with Frontier (NASDAQ:ULCC) Chairman Bill Franke earlier this week, according to a Bloomberg report on Thursday, which cited a person familiar with the matter. 

The talks focused on Spirit's (NYSE:FLYY) rebuilding efforts and the state of the US airline industry, according to the report. There was no talk about an acquisition of Spirit (FLYY). 

Spirit didn’t respond to requests for comment from Bloomberg.

Spirit (FLYY) warned earlier this month that without a cash infusion, it won’t be able to comply with its debt obligations, and is unlikely to survive for another year. 

The talks between Spirit (FLYY) and Frontier (NASDAQ:ULCC) come after Spirit rejected a takeover offer from Frontier in January, following its November bankruptcy filing. 

Shares of Spirit rose 3.2% on Friday.

data.title: Franklin Resources announces launch of Franklin Dividend Growth ETF
data.description:
Franklin Resources (NYSE:BEN) announced on Friday the launch of Franklin Dividend Growth ETF (FRIZ) that aims to invest in U.S. firms with the potential to generate consistent dividend hikes.

The fund invests in companies across the market-cap spectrum and in a diverse range of industries and sectors.

"FRIZ expands our product suite into the large blend category and gives clients another way to access our dividend expertise in a highly diversified portfolio," said David Mann, head of ETF product and capital markets at Franklin Templeton.

data.title: Chicago PMI drops more than expected in August
data.description:
August Chicago PMI: 41.5 vs. 45.3 consensus and 47.1 prior, according to data released by the Institute for Supply Management on Friday.

A reading under 50 indicates a contraction in the Chicago area manufacturing sector.

data.title: Verizon Communications files mixed securities shelf
data.description: Verizon Communications (NYSE:VZ) has filed a registration statement with the SEC for the proposed sale of mixed securities. The company may offer at one or more times common stock, preferred stock, and debt securities.

data.title: Elastic slips even as as analysts praise Q1 results, guidance
data.description:
Elastic (NYSE:ESTC) shares erased premarket gains and fell slightly in early trading on Friday, even as the enterprise software company reported stronger-than-expected results and guidance, leading to praise from Wall Street.

“Elastic reported impressive 1Q26 results with accelerating revenue growth landing ahead of expectations and provided a better than expected guide, sending shares higher after hours,” DA Davison analyst Lucky Schreiner wrote in a note to clients. “Demand for Elastic's AI Search functionality is increasing as AI and Agentic applications are created and security consolidation trends stood out in the quarter. Price increases did contribute to the outperformance, regardless, sales execution continues to improve as Elastic grows with larger enterprise customers.”

Several other investment firms also praised Elastic's results, including Guggenheim and Stifel Nicolaus, both of which raised their price targets on the stock. Guggenheim moved its price target to $122 from $107, while Stifel upped its target to $134 from $112.
